paper_title: Sediment Mesocosm Experiment and Foraminifera Analysis

methods.description: Procedure for setting up and maintaining experimental mesocosms containing sieved sediment and overlying seawater under controlled environmental conditions (light, temperature, CO2, pH, salinity) for 36 weeks.

steps.description: Sediment was collected from the Ythan Estuary, N.E. Scotland (57u209N, 01u579W), in December 2010.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment from specified location

steps.description: Sieved sediment (500 mm), was added to acrylic mesocosms (12612633 cm) to a depth of 12 cm
steps.searchable_summary: sieve sediment to specified size and add to mesocosms

steps.description: and 20 cm of overlying seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: add specified depth of overlying seawater to mesocosms

steps.description: Mesocosms were exposed to a seasonally varying light cycle
steps.searchable_summary: expose mesocosms to a seasonally varying light cycle

steps.description: and maintained at 10uC (average local conditions).
steps.searchable_summary: maintain mesocosm temperature at specified value

steps.description: Seawater pH was controlled by bubbling air with a known concentration of CO 2 (380, 750, 1000 ppm) into each mesocosm [22].
steps.searchable_summary: control seawater pH by bubbling air with specific CO2 concentrations

steps.description: Seawater pH, temperature and salinity were monitored weekly
steps.searchable_summary: monitor seawater pH, temperature, and salinity weekly

steps.description: and atmospheric CO 2 was maintained at 380, 750 or 1000 ppm (Table S1).
steps.searchable_summary: maintain atmospheric CO2 at specified concentrations

steps.description: After 36 weeks of exposure, surface scrapes of 1-2 mm depth were collected from each of the treatments for the isolation of living benthic foraminifera.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface sediment scrapes after 36 weeks

methods.description: Procedure for processing collected sediment scrapes to isolate, stain, and select live benthic foraminifera specimens for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: The surface scrapes were fixed in ethanol
steps.searchable_summary: fix sediment scrapes in ethanol

steps.description: and stained with rose Bengal (to distinguish live individuals).
steps.searchable_summary: stain fixed samples with rose Bengal

steps.description: After 24 h, samples were washed over a 63 mm sieve
steps.searchable_summary: wash stained samples over a sieve after 24 hours

steps.description: and the coarse fraction dried (,40uC).
steps.searchable_summary: dry the coarse sediment fraction at low temperature

steps.description: 15 live adult specimens of Haynesina germanica (300-400 mm), five from each CO 2 treatment, were selected using a fine paintbrush under an OlympusH SZ stereo-microscope
steps.searchable_summary: select live foraminifera specimens under a microscope
inputs: selection criteria (live, adult, Haynesina germanica, 300-400 mm, 5 per treatment)

steps.description: and dry-stored on micropalaeontological slides prior to imaging with a scanning electron microscope (SEM).
steps.searchable_summary: dry-store selected specimens on micropalaeontological slides

methods.description: Procedure for preparing selected foraminifera specimens for SEM imaging and subsequently measuring morphological features (teeth count, length, width) from the images.

steps.description: The foraminiferal specimens were mounted onto SEM stubs using double-sided adhesive tabs.
steps.searchable_summary: mount foraminifera specimens onto SEM stubs

steps.description: Samples were prepared with an EmscopeH SC 500 sputter coater
steps.searchable_summary: sputter coat mounted specimens

steps.description: and imaged with a scanning electronic microscope (JoelH JSM-35CF SEM).
steps.searchable_summary: image specimens using SEM

steps.description: For each specimen, the number of teeth in a 20 mm 2 region of the apertural area were counted
steps.searchable_summary: count teeth in a specific region of SEM images
inputs: region of interest (20 mm² apertural area)

steps.description: and measured (SemAforeH) for maximum length and width (Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: measure maximum length and width from SEM images

methods.description: Statistical procedure used to analyze the effect of different CO2 treatments on measured foraminifera characteristics.

steps.description: Once assumptions were confirmed, a one-way ANOVA was used to determine the effects of CO 2 .
steps.searchable_summary: confirm assumptions for one-way ANOVA

steps.description: a one-way ANOVA was used to determine the effects of CO 2 .
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA to test effect of CO2 treatment

steps.description: Analyses were performed using the statistical programming software 'R' [23].
steps.searchable_summary: execute statistical analysis using R software
